# manydist

![manydist logo](reference/figures/manydist_logo.png)

**Distance-Based Learning for Mixed-Type Data**

`manydist` provides tools for constructing, computing, and using
distance measures for numerical, categorical, and mixed-type data.

  

## Installation

``` r

# install.packages("palmerpenguins")
install.packages("manydist")
```

You can also install the development version from GitHub:

``` r

# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("alfonsoIodiceDE/manydist_package", subdir = "manydist")
```

### Initialization

``` r

library(manydist)
library(palmerpenguins)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
```

## Getting started

The main function in `manydist` is
[`mdist()`](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/reference/mdist.md).
It computes dissimilarities for numerical, categorical, and mixed-type
data.

``` r

penguins_small <- penguins |>
  select(-year) |> drop_na()
```

A mixed-type dissimilarity can be computed with a preset. For example,
`preset = "gower"` computes a Gower-type dissimilarity.

``` r

D <- penguins_small |> select(-species) |> 
  mdist(preset = "gower")

as.matrix(D$distance)[1:5,1:5] |> round(digits=2)
```

``` R
##      1    2    3    4    5
## 1 0.00 0.21 0.25 0.24 0.07
## 2 0.21 0.00 0.07 0.09 0.25
## 3 0.25 0.07 0.00 0.06 0.26
## 4 0.24 0.09 0.06 0.00 0.23
## 5 0.07 0.25 0.26 0.23 0.00
```

Some presets can use a response variable. When a response is supplied,
it is used for response-aware distance construction and is not treated
as an ordinary predictor.

``` r

D_resp <- penguins_small |> mdist(
  response = species,
  preset = "u_dep"
)

D_resp
```

``` R
## MDist object
##   preset : u_dep 
##   number of observations : 333 
##   number of continuous variables   : 4 
##   number of categorical variables   : 2 
##   parameters:
##     - categorical method: tvd
##     - numerical preprocessing: pc_scores
##     - commensurability adjustment: TRUE
```

Custom specifications can be defined by combining a categorical method,
a numerical preprocessing method, and a commensurability rule.

``` r

D_custom <- mdist(penguins_small |> select(-species),
                  preset = "custom",
                  method_cat = "matching",
                  method_num = "std",
                  commensurable = TRUE
)

D_custom
```

``` R
## MDist object
##   preset : custom 
##   number of observations : 333 
##   number of continuous variables   : 4 
##   number of categorical variables   : 2 
##   parameters:
##     - categorical method: matching
##     - numerical preprocessing: std
##     - commensurability adjustment: TRUE
##     - number of principal components: 6
##     - inertia threshold: NULL
```

# Package index

## All functions

- [`all_dist_method_specs()`](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/reference/all_dist_method_specs.md)
  : Create a grid of \`mdist()\` method specifications
- [`benchmark_mdist()`](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/reference/benchmark_mdist.md)
  : Benchmark \`mdist()\` over multiple method specifications
- [`compare_lovo_mdist()`](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/reference/compare_lovo_mdist.md)
  : Compare LOVO diagnostics across multiple distance specifications
- [`congruence_coeff()`](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/reference/congruence_coeff.md)
  : Congruence coefficient between two configurations
- [`dist_methods_tbl()`](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/reference/dist_methods_tbl.md)
  : List available \`manydist\` methods
- [`dist_methods_tbl_cat()`](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/reference/dist_methods_tbl_cat.md)
  : List available categorical dissimilarities
- [`fifa_nl`](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/reference/fifa_nl.md)
  : FIFA 21 Player Data - Dutch League
- [`gen_mixed()`](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/reference/gen_mixed.md)
  : Generate mixed-type clustering data with signal and noise
- [`generate_dataset()`](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/reference/generate_dataset.md)
  : generate toy mixed datasets for the supplementary material: figures
  1 to 3
- [`lovo_mdist()`](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/reference/lovo_mdist.md)
  : Leave-one-variable-out diagnostics for distance-based variable
  importance
- [`lovo_method_spec()`](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/reference/lovo_method_spec.md)
  : Create a LOVO method specification for \`compare_lovo_mdist()\`
- [`make_mdist_recipe()`](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/reference/make_mdist_recipe.md)
  : Build a recipe that computes mdist-based distance features
- [`mdist()`](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/reference/mdist.md)
  : Mixed-type dissimilarities for distance-based learning
- [`mdist_summary_impl()`](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/reference/mdist_summary_impl.md)
  : Internal: summary method implementation for MDist R6 objects
- [`pam_dist()`](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/reference/pam_dist.md)
  : PAM clustering specification based on manydist dissimilarities
- [`response_aware_methods()`](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/reference/response_aware_methods.md)
  : List response-aware methods
- [`spectral_dist()`](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/reference/spectral_dist.md)
  : Spectral clustering specification based on manydist dissimilarities
- [`spectral_from_dist()`](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/reference/spectral_from_dist.md)
  : Spectral clustering from a distance matrix
- [`step_mdist()`](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/reference/step_mdist.md)
  : Add manydist dissimilarities to a recipe

### Distance construction

- [Distance
  specifications](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/articles/choosing_a_distance_specification.md):
- [manydist: distance-based learning with mixed-type
  data](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/articles/understanding_presets.md):

### Diagnostic tools

- [Assessing variable contributions to
  distances](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/articles/variable_contributions.md):
- [manydist: benchmarking distance
  choices](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/articles/benchmarking_distance_choices.md):

### Distance-based learning workflows

- [distance-based clustering
  workflows](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/articles/clustering_workflows.md):
- [supervised learning
  workflows](https://alfonsoiodicede.github.io/manydist_package/articles/knn_workflows.md):
